it: Nella scheda quattro abbiamo introdotto l'equazione di Schrödinger per una particella carica in un potenziale elettrico. Questa equazione ci permette di determinare
en: In card four we introduced the Schrödinger equation for a charged particle in an electric potential. This equation allows us to determine {0}, that is the distribution of probability amplitudes as a function of time for the triple of measurable quantities {1}. The central aim of the present card will be to determine the distribution of probability amplitudes for other observable quantities, in particular for momentum, angular momentum and energy.

it: Per raggiungere lo scopo è necessario rivedere e generalizzare i principi della Meccanica Quantistica che abbiamo postulato nella scheda due.
en: To achieve this aim it is necessary to review and generalise the principles of Quantum Mechanics that we postulated in card two.

it: Principi della Meccanica Quantistica.
en: Principles of Quantum Mechanics.

it: Per comodità di lettura riportiamo qui i principi che abbiamo postulato nella scheda due:
en: For ease of reading we restate here the principles we postulated in card two:

it: Un sistema fisico è caratterizzato dalle sue grandezze fisiche che possono essere misurate.
en: A physical system is characterised by its physical quantities, which can be measured.

it: Quando si misura una grandezza g sono possibili diversi risultati, ed ogni risultato ha una certa probabilità di uscire. Se dalla misura si ottiene un particolare risultato
en: When a quantity g is measured, several outcomes are possible, and each outcome has a certain probability of occurring. If the measurement gives a particular result {0}, the system settles into a state such that, if the measurement of g is repeated, the value {1} is obtained with certainty. Such a state is called an eigenstate of g with eigenvalue {2}.

it: Un qualsiasi altro stato α può essere considerato come una sovrapposizione degli autostati di una grandezza g e può essere rappresentato da un vettore di numeri complessi
en: Any other state α can be considered as a superposition of the eigenstates of a quantity g and can be represented by a vector of complex numbers {0}. Each complex number is associated with one of the possible values that the quantity g can take, {1} The squared modulus of each of these complex numbers, for example α<sub>k</sub>, gives the probability that measuring the quantity g yields the particular value {2} associated with the number considered.

it: In generale se il sistema si trova in uno stato α e si misura una grandezza
en: In general, if the system is in a state α and a quantity {0} is measured, the probability that {1} occurs is given by the squared modulus of the scalar product between the vector {2} and the vector {3} associated with the eigenstate {4} of {5}. {6}

en: The time evolution of a state αt follows a linear law {0}

en: where {0} is a matrix that depends on the system and on the “ambient conditions”. The matrix {1} is called the time-evolution matrix and has the property of leaving scalar products invariant {2}

en: In the second principle the concept of eigenstate is introduced, that is a state such that if a measurement is performed a given value, called the eigenvalue, is obtained with certainty. In the case of a material particle the eigenstates, for example of the quantity x, are those represented by vectors of the form

en: in this case, if a measurement of x, y or z is performed, the values {0}, {1} and {2} will be obtained with certainty; so what we have considered is simultaneously an eigenstate of the quantities x, y and z.

it: I principi che abbiamo dato in realtà sono una versione semplificata, perché si suppone che dato un valore
en: The principles we have given are in fact a simplified version, because it is assumed that, given a value {0}, the eigenstate associated with {1} is unique, which in general is not true; for example in the case of a material particle in three-dimensional space the eigenstates associated with an eigenvalue {2} of x are infinite:

it: Il terzo principio si deve generalizzare dicendo che un qualsiasi stato può essere considerato come la sovrapposizione degli
en: The third principle must be generalised by saying that any state can be considered as the superposition of the “simultaneous eigenstates” of a certain set of physical quantities {0}. The coefficients of this superposition will no longer have a single index but as many as there are observable quantities whose eigenstates are taken, so we will not have {1} but {2}, or in the continuous case we will not have {3} but {4}.

it: Un set di grandezze fisiche
en: A set of physical quantities {0} such that there exists a unique simultaneous eigenstate associated with the eigenvalues {1} will be called “complete”. For example the three position variables x, y and z form a complete set, because given a triple of values {2}, there exists a unique simultaneous eigenstate associated with this triple.

en: The ket associated with the state {0} may be denoted by the symbol

en: however we will prefer to commit an abuse of notation and use a more compact notation, denoting the ket by the symbol {0}. (There is an abuse of notation because if we wanted to substitute numbers, for example {1}, {2} and {3}, we would have the symbol {4} in which values are indicated but the corresponding quantities are not.)

it: Ora consideriamo il quarto principio e supponiamo ad esempio di voler calcolare l'ampiezza di probabilità che, misurando x, y e z contemporaneamente su una particella in uno stato
en: Now let us consider the fourth principle and suppose, for example, that we want to compute the probability amplitude that, measuring x, y and z simultaneously on a particle in a state {0}, the values {1} are obtained; then the sought probability amplitude is given by the scalar product {2} and the probability is given by the squared modulus {3}.

it: A questo punto scriviamo i principi generalizzati
en: At this point we write the generalised principles

it: Un sistema fisico è caratterizzato dalle sue grandezze fisiche che possono essere misurate.
en: A physical system is characterised by its physical quantities, which can be measured.

it: Quando si misura una grandezza g sono possibili diversi risultati, ed ogni risultato ha una certa probabilità di uscire. Se dalla misura si ottiene un particolare risultato
en: When a quantity g is measured, several outcomes are possible, and each outcome has a certain probability of occurring. If the measurement gives a particular result {0}, the system settles into a state such that, if the measurement of g is repeated, the value {1} is obtained with certainty. Such a state is called an eigenstate of g with eigenvalue {2}.

it: 2.bis In generale dato un valore
en: 2.bis In general, given a value {0}, there may exist infinitely many eigenstates associated with this value. However it is always possible to find a complete set of variables {1} such that, given the n values {2}, there exists a unique simultaneous eigenstate associated with these values.

it: Un qualsiasi altro stato α può essere considerato come una sovrapposizione degli autostati simultanei di un set di grandezze
en: Any other state α can be considered as a superposition of the simultaneous eigenstates of a set of quantities {0}, and can be represented by a vector of complex numbers with n indices {1}. Each complex number is associated with one of the possible combinations of values that the n-tuple of quantities {2} can take. The squared modulus of each of these complex numbers, for example {3}, gives the probability that measuring the quantities {4} simultaneously yields the particular set of values {5} and that the system settles into the simultaneous eigenstate {6}.

it: In generale se il sistema si trova in uno stato α e si misura contemporaneamente un set completo di grandezze
en: In general, if the system is in a state α and a complete set of quantities {0} is measured simultaneously, the probability of obtaining {1} is given by the squared modulus of the scalar product between the vector {2} and the vector {3} associated with the simultaneous eigenstate {4}. That is {5}.

en: The generalisation of these principles to the continuous case is immediate: it suffices to replace the index representation {0} with the functional representation {1}.

en: where by I we have denoted the identity matrix. This formula will be extremely useful on many future occasions.

it: Ora supponiamo di voler calcolare la distribuzione di ampiezze di probabilità associata ad altre grandezze fisiche diverse dalla posizione, ad esempio la quantità di moto
en: Now suppose we want to compute the distribution of probability amplitudes associated with physical quantities other than position, for example momentum {0}. (From now on, symbols with an overbar will denote vector quantities.)

it: Ampiezze di probabilità per la quantità di moto.
en: Probability amplitudes for momentum.

it: Se conosciamo lo stato di una particella materiale, e conosciamo la distribuzione di ampiezze di probabilità per la variabile posizione
en: If we know the state of a material particle, and we know the distribution of probability amplitudes for the position variable {0}, how can we determine the distribution of probability amplitudes for the quantity momentum?

it: Se conoscessimo gli autostati della quantità di moto allora potremmo applicare il quarto postulato, ma questi autostati non li conosciamo e li dobbiamo determinare.
en: If we knew the eigenstates of momentum then we could apply the fourth postulate, but we do not know these eigenstates and we must determine them.

it: Per trovare la soluzione al nostro problema dobbiamo prendere l'altro capo della matassa, cioè supponiamo di conoscere quali siano gli autostati della quantità di moto, e da questi cerchiamo di calcolare un valore che già conosciamo per altra via, così potremo avere un legame tra le cose che sappiamo e quelle che non abbiamo ancora determinato.
en: To find the solution to our problem we must take the other end of the thread, that is we suppose we know what the eigenstates of momentum are, and from these we try to compute a value that we already know by another route, so that we can have a link between the things we know and those we have not yet determined.

it: Supponiamo ad esempio di voler calcolare il valore medio della componente lungo x della quantità di moto
en: Suppose for example that we want to compute the mean value of the component along x of the momentum {0}, assuming we know the state of the system {1} and the eigenstates of momentum.

en: Now we generalise the results obtained for momentum with the following very important theorem:

Therefore it is not possible to perform a precise and simultaneous measurement of the quantities {2} and {3}, whereas it is perfectly possible in other cases, for example with the quantities {4}, {5}.

it: L'energia.
en: Energy.

it: Come ultima grandezza di studio consideriamo l'energia.
en: As the last quantity of study we consider energy.

it: Nel formalismo della Meccanica Classica, per una particella carica in un potenziale elettrico, abbiamo l'energia

en: Therefore, if we want to find the values that energy can take, and the eigenstates into which the particle settles after a measurement of energy, then we must solve the following eigenvalue problem:

it: Livelli energetici per l'atomo di idrogeno.
en: Energy levels for the hydrogen atom.

it: L'atomo di idrogeno è costituito da un nucleo con carica pari a quella dell'elettrone, e da un unico elettrone orbitante.
en: The hydrogen atom consists of a nucleus with charge equal to that of the electron, and a single orbiting electron.

it: Per determinare il moto dell'elettrone attorno al nucleo si deve risolvere l'equazione di Schrödinger, mentre se si vogliono determinare i valori che può assumere l'energia dell'atomo, basta risolvere il problema agli autovalori che abbiamo posto nel precedente paragrafo nel caso in cui abbiamo un potenziale columbiano
en: To determine the motion of the electron around the nucleus one must solve the Schrödinger equation, whereas if one wants to determine the values that the energy of the atom can take, it suffices to solve the eigenvalue problem we posed in the previous paragraph in the case where we have a Coulomb potential {0}.

it: In questa scheda non vogliamo risolvere questi complicati problemi matematici, e rimandiamo lo svolgimento ad una scheda futura, tuttavia vogliamo anticipare i risultati per i livelli energetici:
en: In this card we do not wish to solve these complicated mathematical problems, and we defer the treatment to a future card; nevertheless we wish to anticipate the results for the energy levels:
